title: Avoid Gratuitous Boolean Expressions
description: Boolean expressions that do not change the program's evaluation are known as gratuitous boolean expressions. These can suggest logical errors, impact the application's reliability, and hinder maintainability. Care should be taken to ensure the condition in control flow constructs like if-statements is not always true or false to prevent unnecessary branching.
recommendation: Ensure the conditions in control flow constructs like if-statements are not gratuitously true or false to prevent unnecessary branching and potential logical errors.
impact: Gratuitous boolean expressions can result in logical errors, incorrect computations, and hinder code readability and maintainability.

checks.message: Ensure the conditions in control flow constructs like if-statements are not gratuitously true or false to prevent unnecessary branching and potential logical errors.
